meaning: vi.(猪等)作呼噜声,(表示烦恼、反对、疲劳、轻蔑等)发哼声vt.咕哝着说出n.呼噜声,咕哝
examples: Clive made a noise, something like a grunt.
It was not an unfriendly grunt, but it was a grunt.
"Tha' might sing th' Doxology," he suggested in his dryest grunt.
Make no mistake, much of what we both did was grunt work: boring, mindless, repetitious.
That's not to say that a high-pitched rough grunt means, "Hey, everyone, check out the bread!"
